rulings.Case Title: DIANE RICHARDS VS. BUREAU OF AUTOMOTIVE REPAIR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute; Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Justin R. Surber
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 08, 2025, Line 1. RESPONDENT BUREAU OF AUTOMOTIVE REPAIR, DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute. Respondent Bureau of Automotive Repair's unopposed Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute is GRANTED. The motion is brought under Code of Civil Procedure sections 583.310 and 583.360(a). "An action shall be brought to trial within five years after the action is commenced against the defendant." (Code Civil Procedure section 583.310.) "An action shall be dismissed by the court on its own motion or on motion of the defendant, after notice to the parties, if the action is not brought to trial within the time prescribed in this article." (Code Civil Procedure section 583.360(a.) The rules apply to special proceedings, such as this one. (See Binyon v. State of Cal. (1993) 17 Cal.App.4th 952, 955.) The record here demonstrates that Petitioner failed to bring this case to trial within the time prescribed by Article 1 of Chapter 1.5 of the Code of Civil Procedure, even accounting for the tolling period provided in California Rules of Court, app. Emergency Rule 9. The motion, thus, is granted, and the court orders the petition dismissed with prejudice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: BOLUWATIFE AIKI-RAJI ET AL VS. TWITTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss The Complaint For Forum Non Conveniens Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 08, 2025, Line 8. DEFENDANT X CORP.'s Motion To Dismiss The Complaint For Forum Non Conveniens Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Stay. Defendant X Corporation's Motion To Dismiss The Complaint For Forum Non Conveniens Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Stay is continued to June 30, 2025, to provide the parties time to file and the court to decide an application for complex designation under California Rules of Court 3.400. Upon preliminary review of the case and the instant motion, it appears the case may be appropriate for designation. It involves sixty-seven Plaintiffs, could require management of a large number of witnesses or a substantial amount of documentary evidence, and most importantly could require "coordination with related actions pending in one or more courts in other counties, states, or countries, or in federal court." (CRC 3.400(b)(4).) Similar claims are set to be decided in a federal class action transferred to Delaware from California District Court. (Cornet, et al. v. Twitter, Inc., Case No. 3:22-cv-06857-JD.) Another class action case filed in this court, which is designated complex, makes similar claims. (Rebekah Justice, et al. v. Twitter, Inc., CGC-23-604287.) That case is stayed pending resolution of the federal class action. It appears that this case could benefit from increased judicial management. No later than June 20, 2025, the parties shall file a joint statement regarding application for complex designation under California Rules of Court 3.400 discussing their efforts and the status of any application. Defendant X Corporation shall provide a courtesy copy of such filing to this department within one business day of submission for filing. Defendant X Corporation shall submit a proposed order setting out the above text verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: YESSICA GARCIA CÁRDENAS VS. AIRBNB, INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 08, 2025, Line 9. 2 - DEFENDANTs AIRBNB, INC, AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC.,and AIRBNB TRAVEL, LLC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants Airbnb Payments, Inc. and Airbnb Travel LLC's Motion to Strike is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. "Any party, within the time allowed to respond to a pleading may serve and file a notice of motion to strike the whole or any part thereof" (CCP 435.) "The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: [para] (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (CCP 436.) "In the construction of a pleading, for the purpose of determining its effect, its allegations must be liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (CCP 452.) As to the prayer for decedent's lost earnings, Plaintiff concedes that the request is improper. (Opposition, 5:27-28.) Thus, the court grants the motion and the prayer for decedent's lost earnings is ordered stricken without leave to amend. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: BENNY IGNACIO VS. US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 08, 2025, Line 10. DEFENDANT US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Defendant US Bank National Association's Motion to Compel Arbitration and Stay Further Proceedings is GRANTED. Defendant has established Plaintiff Benny Ignacio agreed to arbitrate with Defendant and the claims in the Complaint appear to be covered by the arbitration agreement; Plaintiff presented no evidence to the contrary and did not present evidence or argument in support of a defense against enforcement of the arbitration agreement. (See Gamboa v. Northeast Community Clinic (2021) 72 Cal.App.5th 158, 165.) The court, thus, grants the motion and orders Plaintiff to arbitrate his claims against Defendant. Further proceedings in this action are stayed pending the arbitration. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.4; see also 9 U.S.C. section 3.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
